title: meshcore_adapter.c: compat_valid_utf8 missing test for 0xc0-0xc1 range (overlong)
description:
The UTF-8 validation function compat_valid_utf8() correctly handles most edge cases but allows bytes 0xc0 and 0xc1 which are overlong encodings (they would encode ASCII chars < 0x80 using 2 bytes). RFC 3629 explicitly forbids these. The check 'c >= 0xc2U' is correct for the start, but the else branch falls through to return false which catches this. Confirmed correct - no issue.

Actually, reviewing again: the code has 'else if (c >= 0xc2U && c <= 0xdfU)' which correctly skips 0xc0-0xc1 (they fall through to the final 'return false'). Closing as not-a-bug.

File: lichen/apps/gateway/src/meshcore_adapter.c lines 105-140
